title: RGAA — Référentiel Général d'Amélioration de l'Accessibilité (DINUM)
insights: Official French national accessibility standard. 106 criteria based on WCAG 2.1. Current version RGAA 4.1.2. Mandatory for French public sector sites. Penalties up to €50,000 since Jan 2024 for non-compliance. RGAA v5 planned for end 2026.

title: European Accessibility Act (EAA) — European Commission
insights: EAA became law in all EU member states June 28 2025. Covers all digital products/services with a digital interface. Uses EN 301 549 as presumptive standard for conformance. WCAG 2.1 AA embedded in EN 301 549 v3.2.1. Penalties up to €100k or 4% annual revenue. Exempts companies <10 employees.

title: dequelabs/axe-core — GitHub (7k stars)
insights: Core engine with 7000+ stars. Catches 57% of WCAG issues automatically. Zero false positives philosophy. Supports WCAG 2.0, 2.1, 2.2 at A/AA/AAA. Shadow DOM support. Used by Lighthouse, Chrome DevTools, jest-axe, @axe-core/playwright. Min release cycle 3-5 months.

title: Inclusive Components by Heydon Pickering
insights: Pattern library covering accordions, tables, tabs, toggle buttons, modals, notifications, sliders. Treats every component through the lens of inclusion. Detailed ARIA + keyboard analysis. Book form also available.

title: The WebAIM Million 2024 Report
insights: 95.9% of home pages have detectable WCAG failures. 56.8 errors per page (up from 50 in 2023). Top 6 errors unchanged for 5 years: low contrast (81%), missing alt text (54.5%), missing form labels (48.6%), empty links (44.6%), empty buttons, missing lang. Pages with ARIA present average 41% MORE errors.

title: WebAIM Screen Reader User Survey #10 (2024)
insights: JAWS 41%, NVDA 38% as primary. Chrome 52%, Edge 19%, Firefox 16% with screen readers. 91.3% use screen reader on mobile; VoiceOver dominates mobile at 70.6%. Only 34.6% say web accessibility improved (down from 39.3% in 2021). Headings remain top navigation method.

title: EN 301 549 V3 — ETSI Harmonized European Standard for ICT Accessibility
insights: EN 301 549 V3.2.1 (2021) includes full WCAG 2.1 text. EN 301 549 v4.1.1 planned for 2026 to support EAA and include WCAG 2.2 AA. Produced by CEN, CENELEC, ETSI jointly. Used for EAA conformance.

title: Sara Soueidan — Blog
insights: Top accessibility engineering blog. 80+ articles on HTML, CSS, SVG, ARIA, accessibility. Recent 2025: CSS carousels accessibility analysis, scroll-marker-group, CSS alt text syntax. Deep technical depth, no fluff.

title: Accessible notifications with ARIA Live Regions Part 1 — Sara Soueidan
insights: Detailed two-part series. Key: pre-render two live region containers on page load (one assertive, one polite), inject messages into them via JS. Avoids race conditions. role=alert vs role=status vs aria-live distinctions explained precisely.

title: Accessible notifications with ARIA Live Regions Part 2 — Sara Soueidan
insights: Part 2: practical implementation patterns. Covers aria-atomic, message queue management, toast notification accessibility trade-offs (avoid auto-dismiss for critical messages). Excerpt from Practical Accessibility course.

title: Web Content Accessibility Guidelines (WCAG) 2.2 — W3C Recommendation
insights: The canonical standard. 87 success criteria across A/AA/AAA. 9 new criteria added vs WCAG 2.1 including 2.4.11 Focus Not Obscured, 2.4.13 Focus Appearance, 2.5.7 Dragging, 2.5.8 Target Size, 3.2.6 Consistent Help, 3.3.7 Redundant Entry, 3.3.8 Accessible Auth. Became W3C Recommendation October 5 2023. 4.1.1 Parsing removed as obsolete.

title: Accessible Rich Internet Applications (WAI-ARIA) 1.2 — W3C
insights: Full ARIA 1.2 specification. Combobox pattern updated significantly from 1.1. New roles: comment, mark, suggestion, cell, row, rowgroup. Role taxonomy and inheritance model. ARIA 1.3 draft in development.

title: ARIA Authoring Practices Guide (APG) — WAI/W3C
insights: 60+ component patterns with ARIA roles, states, props, and keyboard interaction specs. Ground truth for widget implementation. Accordion, Combobox, Dialog, Tabs, Tree all covered. Community-driven; actively updated in 2024.

title: Landmark Regions — WAI-ARIA APG Practices
insights: header, nav, main, aside, footer as landmark types. Multiple nav elements need aria-label. All content must be inside a landmark. Replaces skip-link as primary navigation method for screen readers.

title: Understanding WCAG 2.2 — WAI/W3C
insights: Official Understanding documents for all 87 WCAG 2.2 success criteria. Each page: intent, benefits, examples, techniques, failures. The definitive reference behind the spec text. Use for any ambiguous criterion.

title: What's New in WCAG 2.2 — WAI/W3C
insights: Official diff document listing all 9 new success criteria with rationale. Essential starting point for upgrading from WCAG 2.1 AA compliance. Confirms 4.1.1 Parsing is obsolete.

title: The A11Y Project Accessibility Checklist
insights: Community-driven WCAG-based checklist organized by area: content, global code, images, headings, lists, controls, tables, forms. Practical implementation guide. Widely used as QA checklist.

title: Kit d'audit RGAA — accessibilite.numerique.gouv.fr
insights: Official DINUM audit kit. Spreadsheet templates for RGAA 4.1 full audit (106 criteria) and simplified audit. Test methodology for each criterion. Essential for French public sector compliance.

title: Ara — DINUM Accessibility Audit Tool
insights: Free French government tool for conducting RGAA audits manually. Generates RGAA audit reports and accessibility declarations. NOT automated — requires expert knowledge of 106 RGAA criteria. Built by DINUM.

title: Assistiv Labs — Cloud Screen Reader Testing
insights: Real screen readers (NVDA, JAWS, VoiceOver, Narrator, ZoomText) in cloud. Run on Mac/PC/Linux. Can automate screen reader interactions. Finds 75% of bugs that automated checkers miss. Used by Asana for E2E a11y testing.

title: Which Screen Readers and Browsers are Best for Accessibility Testing? — Assistiv Labs
insights: JAWS+Chrome, NVDA+Chrome, JAWS+Edge, NVDA+Firefox are the dominant pairings. 90% of usage split across NVDA+JAWS+VoiceOver. Recommends testing NVDA+Chrome + VoiceOver+Safari minimum.

title: prefers-reduced-motion — CSS-Tricks
insights: Comprehensive almanac entry. Syntax, values, real examples. Integration with GSAP and other animation libraries. System settings that trigger it (Windows Reduce Motion, macOS Reduce Motion, iOS, Android).

title: Deque University — Web Accessibility Training
insights: Self-paced courses $45/year each. 2 fundamentals + 12 web accessibility courses. 60-90 min average. IAAP CPACC/WAS exam preparation. Role-based recommendations. Includes WCAG 2.2 resources and testing methodology.

title: WCAG 2.2 Updates — Deque University Resources
insights: Deque's WCAG 2.2 reference page with code examples. Organized by new success criteria. Practical implementation guidance beyond the W3C spec language.

title: List of Axe 4.10 Rules — Deque University
insights: Complete rule catalog for axe-core 4.10 including WCAG tag, impact level (critical/serious/moderate/minor), and description. Best reference for understanding what is and isn't automatically detectable.

title: Lighthouse Accessibility Score — Chrome for Developers
insights: Weighted average of all axe-based audits. Pass/fail only — no partial credit. Set baseline >90 in LHCI config. Lighthouse 12 (May 2024) removed PWA category. LHCI 0.15.x uses Lighthouse 12.6.1.

title: Accessibility in React — MDN Learn
insights: React-specific a11y guide: focus management after DOM updates, managing focus with refs/useEffect, keyboard trap in modals, ESLint jsx-a11y integration, routing focus management with react-router.

title: ARIA — Accessibility — MDN Web Docs
insights: MDN's ARIA hub. Covers roles, states, properties, live regions, techniques. Key principle: semantic HTML before ARIA. ARIA on div does NOT make it keyboard-interactive. Foundational reference.

title: ARIA live regions — MDN Web Docs
insights: aria-live='polite' waits for user idle. aria-live='assertive' interrupts immediately (use sparingly). aria-atomic=true announces entire region. aria-relevant for specific change types. role=alert = assertive + atomic. role=status = polite. Have only 2 live regions per page (one each).

title: ARIA: aria-current attribute — MDN Web Docs
insights: Values: page, step, location, date, time, true. aria-current='page' for breadcrumbs and nav items. Announced by screen readers as 'current'. Distinct from aria-selected (used in list/tree contexts).

title: ARIA: tablist role — MDN Web Docs
insights: role=tablist container, role=tab for each tab, role=tabpanel for content. Active tab tabindex=0, inactive tabindex=-1. Arrow keys between tabs. Tab key enters panel. Home/End to first/last.

title: ARIA: tooltip role — MDN Web Docs
insights: role=tooltip + aria-describedby on trigger. Visible on hover and keyboard focus. Escape dismisses. Do not use HTML title attribute (inconsistently supported). WCAG 1.4.13: content visible on hover must be persistent and hoverable.

title: prefers-reduced-motion — MDN Web Docs CSS
insights: Media query detecting OS-level reduced motion preference. Values: no-preference, reduce. 70M+ people have vestibular disorders triggered by animation. Apply to every animation. Pause button still needed for >5s animations. No-motion-first approach recommended.

title: Using Media Queries for Accessibility — MDN CSS
insights: Hub for all accessibility media queries: prefers-reduced-motion, prefers-color-scheme, prefers-contrast, forced-colors, inverted-colors. Multiple queries often used together for robust support.

title: adobe/react-spectrum — GitHub
insights: Full monorepo including React Aria, React Spectrum, React Stately. Adobe's production accessible component system. Built to WCAG + WAI-ARIA APG patterns. Cross-browser AT normalization built in.

title: eslint-plugin-jsx-a11y — GitHub
insights: Static AST checker for JSX a11y rules. plugin:jsx-a11y/recommended for standard rules, /strict for stricter subset. Catches missing alt text, invalid ARIA roles, missing form labels at lint time. Use alongside axe-core — static + dynamic coverage.

title: jest-axe — Custom Jest Matcher for axe-core
insights: toHaveNoViolations Jest custom matcher. Wraps axe-core for unit tests. Color contrast checks disabled in JSDOM (known limitation). Add toHaveNoViolations to every component test for regression prevention. Color contrast must be tested separately in browser.

title: w3c/aria-practices — GitHub (WAI-ARIA APG source)
insights: Source code for the APG. Active 2024 updates: forced-colors media query additions, radio group Enter key removal, combobox aria-selected alignment, scrollable listbox improvements. Task force weekly calls.

title: Accessibility Testing — Playwright Docs
insights: Official Playwright + axe-core integration. @axe-core/playwright package. AxeBuilder API. checkA11y() in tests. Snapshots for violation tracking. Integration into CI/CD straightforward.

title: Practical Accessibility — Sara Soueidan Online Course
insights: Professional online course covering accessible UI components and digital products. By Sara Soueidan, widely regarded as one of the top accessibility educators. Deep dives on CSS, ARIA, and component patterns.

title: React Aria — Adobe
insights: 50+ hooks implementing WAI-ARIA patterns. Normalizes browser/AT differences. Full keyboard and screen reader support. Style-agnostic — works with Tailwind, CSS modules, etc. usePress, useFocusRing, useInteractOutside, useFocusWithin hooks highly reusable.

title: @storybook/addon-a11y — Storybook Addons
insights: NPM package for Storybook accessibility tab. Runs axe-core on rendered story DOM. Config: disable rule across all stories with enabled:false in preview.js. Toolbar icon + Accessibility panel UI.

title: Accessibility Tests — Storybook 8 Docs
insights: @storybook/addon-a11y uses axe-core. Integrates with Test addon to run automatically on component render. Story-level manual:true to skip. Config via parameters.a11y.config.rules. Violations appear in sidebar and fail CI.

title: Accessibility Testing with Storybook — Storybook Tutorials
insights: Full tutorial: install addon, configure axe rules, write stories, view panel results, integrate with CI. Shows how a11y gate in Storybook prevents regressions per component.

title: Design Patterns and WCAG — TetraLogical (Aug 2024)
insights: Clarifies that APG patterns are best-practice guidance, NOT WCAG conformance requirements. WCAG is technology-neutral — the how (ARIA vs native HTML) is flexible as long as the outcome is accessible.

title: Foundations: Form Validation and Error Messages — TetraLogical (Oct 2024)
insights: Accessible errors: aria-invalid='true' on field, aria-describedby linking to error message. Error summary at top with links for WCAG 2.2. In-line errors appear after focus leaves field. Text-based, not color-only.

title: WAVE Web Accessibility Evaluation Tools — WebAIM
insights: Free browser extension + API for accessibility evaluation. Visual overlay showing errors, alerts, structural elements, ARIA. 1M+ pages scanned monthly via Pope Tech. Complementary to axe-core — different rule emphasis.

title: Accessibility audit with react-axe and eslint-plugin-jsx-a11y — web.dev
insights: Google's guide on layered a11y tooling: eslint-plugin-jsx-a11y (static) + @axe-core/react (runtime in browser) + jest-axe (test time). Three layers catch different issue types. Essential shift-left strategy.

title: WebAIM Contrast Checker
insights: Free tool for manual color contrast verification. WCAG AA requires 4.5:1 normal text, 3:1 large text. WCAG AAA requires 7:1 and 4.5:1. Color contrast is the #1 accessibility violation (83.6% of sites).

title: WebAIM: Introduction to ARIA
insights: Foundational ARIA tutorial. Key rule: no ARIA is better than bad ARIA. Semantic HTML first, ARIA as enhancement. ARIA does not make elements keyboard-focusable automatically.

title: WebAIM: Skip Navigation Links
insights: Skip link must be first item in page. Target is main content area by id. Visually hide off-screen with CSS, position on-screen on focus. role=main is the programmatic alternative for screen readers.

title: WebAIM: Creating Accessible Tables — Data Tables
insights: th with scope='col'/'row', caption element, id+headers for complex tables. Screen reader announces header before cell data. Avoid layout tables. Flatten complex multi-level headers where possible.

title: Practical HTML, CSS, ARIA & JS Tips for Accessible Mega Menus — A11Y Collective
insights: Do NOT use role='menu'/role='menuitem' for navigation — hides links from link list shortcuts. Use nav+ul+li. aria-expanded on trigger. Click (not hover) to open. Escape closes. Return focus to trigger on close.

title: The Complete Guide to ARIA Live Regions — A11Y Collective
insights: Comprehensive live regions guide. Covers all values, aria-atomic, aria-relevant, timing pitfalls. Key warning: inserting content into live region immediately at page load may not be announced. Pre-render container, inject message later.

title: Accessibility Overlay Widgets Attract Lawsuits — Accessibility.Works
insights: Technical analysis of why overlays fail. Even the best automated software catches only 30% of WCAG guidelines. Overlays often introduce NEW barriers. Real remediation requires manual code fixes.

title: The Anatomy of Accessible Forms: Error Messages — Deque
insights: aria-invalid='true', aria-describedby pointing to error. Error text must be next to or linked to the field. Do not rely on color alone. Error summary at top with anchor links to fields. Deque's canonical guide.

title: RGAA Compliance: Complete Guide to French Digital Accessibility 2025 — DigitalA11Y
insights: RGAA 4.1.2 = 106 criteria based on WCAG 2.1. Less than 10% of French sites fully compliant in 2024. Penalties up to €50k since Jan 2024. Accessibility declaration mandatory. Private sector obligations extended from June 2025 (EAA transposition).

title: VPAT — Information Technology Industry Council (ITI)
insights: VPAT 2.5 (2023) aligned to WCAG 2.2. 4 editions: 508, EU, WCAG, INT. Completed VPAT = Accessibility Conformance Report (ACR). Required for US federal procurement. Enterprise accessibility due diligence.

title: New Class Action Lawsuit against AccessiBe — Law Office of Lainey Feingold (Jul 2024)
insights: July 2024 class action. AccessiBe overlay failed to provide compliance despite claims. FTC fined AccessiBe $1M Jan 2025 for false advertising. 25% of all ADA lawsuits in 2024 cited overlays as barriers. Critical warning against overlay-as-compliance.

title: Accessibility — Radix Primitives
insights: Radix follows WAI-ARIA APG patterns. All primitives include aria attributes, keyboard navigation, focus management. Tested across modern browsers and assistive technologies. Used as base for shadcn/ui.

title: Windows High Contrast Mode, Forced Colors & CSS Custom Properties — Smashing Magazine
insights: Forced-colors mode overrides CSS. System color pairs: ButtonFace/ButtonText, Canvas/CanvasText. -ms-high-contrast deprecated in Edge 2024. Use CSS system colors for HCM support. Background images get hidden — use content or text fallbacks.

title: A Step-By-Step Guide to Building Accessible Carousels — Smashing Magazine
insights: Comprehensive implementation guide. aria-roledescription, pause controls mandatory for auto-advance, aria-hidden on inactive slides. Semantic HTML + CSS scroll for performance. Covers WCAG 2.5.2 (no activation on pointer release) considerations.

title: WCAG 3.0 Proposed Scoring Model: A Shift in Accessibility Evaluation — Smashing Magazine
insights: Deep analysis of WCAG 3.0 scoring vs WCAG 2.x binary model. WCAG 3.0 prioritizes usability over compliance. Shift from 'presence of features' to 'quality of access'. Not backward compatible. Important for strategic planning.

title: Accessibility — Smashing Magazine
insights: Ongoing articles from top practitioners. 2024 highlights: 'Designer's Accessibility Advocacy Toolkit' (Feb 2024), 'Conducting Accessibility Research in an Inaccessible Ecosystem' (Apr 2024). 2025: WCAG 3.0 scoring model analysis.

title: prefers-reduced-motion: No-Motion-First Approach — Tatiana Mac
insights: Flips the pattern: no-motion as default, add animation in @media (prefers-reduced-motion: no-preference). More inclusive than adding @media reduce as an afterthought. Widely cited approach in a11y community.

title: New Success Criteria in WCAG 2.2 — TPGi (Vispero)
insights: Deep technical analysis of all 9 new SC. Steve Faulkner contributor analysis. Includes implementation notes and edge cases for each criterion.

title: Making Content Usable for People with Cognitive and Learning Disabilities — W3C
insights: COGA supplemental guidance. 8 objectives beyond WCAG AA. Covers cognitive load, consistent navigation, help for errors, no unusual words. WCAG 2.2 AA weak for cognitive disabilities — COGA fills the gap. Used in WCAG 3.0 development.

title: Accordion Pattern — WAI-ARIA APG
insights: Disclosure/accordion canonical pattern. Button with aria-expanded controls sibling div. aria-controls links trigger to panel. Enter/Space activate. No role='menu' misuse.

title: Breadcrumb Pattern — WAI-ARIA APG
insights: nav element with aria-label='Breadcrumb'. Ordered list structure. Current page link has aria-current='page'. Visual separators via CSS only (not in DOM) to avoid screen reader verbosity.

title: Breadcrumb Example — WAI-ARIA APG
insights: Live implementation. nav aria-label='Breadcrumb' wraps ol. aria-current='page' on last link. CSS-only separators via ::after or content. Accessible in JAWS, NVDA, VoiceOver.

title: Carousel (Slide Show) Pattern — WAI-ARIA APG
insights: aria-roledescription='carousel' on container. aria-live='polite' or aria-atomic for slide changes. Pause button REQUIRED for auto-advancing. aria-hidden on inactive slides.

title: Combobox Pattern — WAI-ARIA APG
insights: Complex pattern — ARIA 1.2 combobox has two forms: editable (input+listbox) and select-only. aria-expanded, aria-autocomplete, aria-activedescendant are the key attributes. ARIA 1.1 and 1.2 patterns differ significantly.

title: Editable Combobox With List Autocomplete — WAI-ARIA APG
insights: Reference implementation for autocomplete combobox. aria-autocomplete='list', aria-activedescendant tracks active option. Down Arrow moves to list. Escape clears/closes. Essential for search inputs and select-with-search patterns.

title: Dialog (Modal) Pattern — WAI-ARIA APG
insights: role='dialog', aria-modal='true', aria-labelledby. Focus moves to dialog on open, returns to trigger on close. Escape closes. Tab cycles within dialog (focus trap). aria-describedby for dialog description. HTML dialog element is modern native alternative.

title: Date Picker Dialog Example — WAI-ARIA APG
insights: Calendar grid uses role=grid, grid cells use role=gridcell. Page keys change month. Arrow keys navigate days. Escape closes. Heading is a live region for month/year changes.

title: Listbox Pattern — WAI-ARIA APG
insights: role='listbox', role='option'. aria-selected tracks selection. Multi-select uses aria-multiselectable. Arrow keys navigate, Space selects. Different from select element — avoid unless native HTML can't serve the need.

title: Tabs Pattern — WAI-ARIA APG
insights: role=tablist, role=tab, role=tabpanel. Arrow keys navigate tabs, Tab enters panel. tabindex=-1 for inactive tabs. Auto-activation recommended unless content load is slow.

title: Tabs with Manual Activation Example — WAI-ARIA APG
insights: Reference implementation of manual-activation tabs. Arrow keys move focus but do NOT activate. Enter/Space activates. Use when tab panels contain async-loaded content. Compare with auto-activation example.

title: Tooltip Pattern — WAI-ARIA APG
insights: Official pattern. Tooltip appears on focus AND hover. Tooltip itself is NOT focusable. aria-describedby links trigger to tooltip. Dismiss with Escape. Persistent on hover (WCAG 1.4.13). Popover for interactive content.

title: WAI-ARIA APG — Practices section
insights: Covers landmark regions, keyboard conventions, hiding semantics, structural roles — the practices that cut across all component patterns.

title: C39: Using prefers-reduced-motion to prevent motion — WCAG Techniques
insights: Official WCAG technique for WCAG 2.3.3 Animation from Interactions (AAA). CSS technique demonstrating correct use of the media query to respect user preference.

title: Understanding SC 3.3.8: Accessible Authentication (Minimum) — WAI/W3C
insights: AA level. Cognitive function tests (memorize password, solve puzzle) not required unless alternative exists or mechanism to assist. Paste from clipboard into OTP fields required. Show-password toggle recommended to reduce cognitive load.

title: Understanding SC 3.2.6: Consistent Help — WAI/W3C
insights: A level. Help mechanisms (contact info, chat, FAQ) must appear in same relative order across all pages where present. Serialized order in DOM matters — not just visual. Benefits neurodivergent users navigating complex processes.

title: Understanding SC 2.5.7: Dragging Movements — WAI/W3C
insights: AA level. All drag functionality must have a single-pointer alternative (e.g., click button instead of drag-to-delete, text input alongside slider). Only start and end point matters — not the path. Differs from 2.5.1 Pointer Gestures.

title: Understanding SC 2.4.13: Focus Appearance — WAI/W3C
insights: AAA level. Focus indicator must be at least 2 CSS px thick perimeter. 3:1 contrast ratio between focused/unfocused states. Never use outline:none without equally prominent custom indicator.

title: Understanding SC 2.4.11: Focus Not Obscured (Minimum) — WAI/W3C
insights: AA level. Focused element must not be COMPLETELY hidden by sticky headers, cookie banners, modals. At least part must remain visible. CSS scroll-padding is the fix for sticky header overlap. scroll-padding-top = height of sticky header.

title: Understanding SC 2.4.7: Focus Visible — WAI/W3C
insights: AA level (WCAG 2.1). Focus indicator must be visible — browser default is acceptable but often insufficient. 2.4.13 (AAA) adds specifics: 2px thick, 3:1 contrast. Never remove outline without replacement.

title: Understanding SC 3.3.7: Redundant Entry — WAI/W3C
insights: A level. Multi-step forms must not re-ask previously entered info unless essential (security, invalid data). Auto-populate or present as selectable option. Does not apply across sessions.

title: Understanding SC 2.5.8: Target Size (Minimum) — WAI/W3C
insights: AA level. Interactive targets must be at least 24x24 CSS pixels or have sufficient spacing. Inline text links exempt. Best practice: 44x44px (matches Apple and Material Design recommendations).

title: Cognitive Accessibility at W3C — WAI
insights: Hub page for COGA task force resources, WCAG 2.2 cognitive criteria, and planned WCAG 3.0 cognitive outcomes. Links to 'Making Content Usable' and pattern library.

title: User Notification (Forms) — WAI/W3C Tutorial
insights: In-page notification for form errors. On-submit: focus error summary, list links to each invalid field. aria-invalid marks fields. role=alert for immediate errors. Official W3C tutorial.

title: An Alt Decision Tree — WAI/W3C
insights: Decision tree for choosing correct alt text: decorative (alt=''), functional (describe purpose), informative (describe content), complex (long description needed), text in images (transcribe). Essential for content authors and developers.

title: Headings — WAI Page Structure Tutorial
insights: Heading hierarchy as page outline. One H1 per page. No skipped levels. 67.5% of SR users navigate by headings (WebAIM SR survey). Visual styling must not drive heading choice. Heading = semantic level, not size.

title: Tables Tutorial — WAI/W3C
insights: Progressive complexity: simple (scope), multi-level (headers attribute), irregular (id+headers). caption for table title. scope='col'/'row'/'colgroup'/'rowgroup'. All th should have scope.

title: The A11Y Project — Home
insights: Community effort to make digital accessibility easier. Resources, checklist, webring for practitioners. Covers all roles: developers, designers, content authors.

title: WebAIM Screen Reader Survey #10: Key Takeaways 2024 — Allyant
insights: Survey analysis: headings remain top navigation method. Only 34.6% report a11y improvement (declining trend). Importance of real-user testing beyond automated checks. Planning matrix for testing priorities.

title: RGAA: New Accessibility Rules Coming in 2025 — ATI4Group
insights: Analysis of RGAA evolution. Private sector obligations from June 2025 (EAA transposition). RGAA v5 planned end 2026. Key for planning 2025-2026 compliance roadmaps for French private companies.

title: Générateur de Déclaration d'Accessibilité — BetaGouv/DINUM
insights: Free tool from French government (BetaGouv) to generate the mandatory accessibility declaration (déclaration d'accessibilité) required under French law. Required for public sector and now extended to private sector.

title: Build an Accessible Modal with focus-trap-react — LogRocket
insights: focus-trap-react library wraps modal for keyboard trap. Must also add ARIA: role='dialog', aria-modal='true', aria-labelledby. Return focus to trigger button on close. HTML dialog element showModal() as modern alternative.

title: Headless UI alternatives: Radix vs React Aria vs Ark UI — LogRocket
insights: Comparison matrix. Radix: mature, ready-to-use. React Aria: maximum control, hook-based. Ark UI: newer, Zag.js state machines. All implement WAI-ARIA. Choose React Aria if a11y is primary concern.

title: List of Axe 4.9 Rules — Deque University
insights: Version before 4.10. AAA rules disabled by default. Rules under development also disabled. Good for understanding evolution of rule coverage.

title: Make Accessible Carousels — Chrome for Developers
insights: Modern approach using CSS scroll-state and scroll-marker-group. Performance-first carousels that are accessible at first paint. Combining semantic HTML, CSS interactivity, and ARIA for progressively enhanced carousels.

title: ARIA: aria-busy attribute — MDN Web Docs
insights: aria-busy=true signals content loading. Do NOT use on buttons (inconsistent screen reader support). Use with aria-live region for spinner state. Set to false when content loads. Pair with role=status for polite announcement.

title: prefers-contrast — MDN Web Docs CSS
insights: Values: more, less, custom, no-preference. Different from forced-colors. Use to enhance color differentiation for elements that use color-only distinction. Microsoft deprecated -ms-high-contrast in Edge 2024 in favor of standard forced-colors.

title: DISIC/Ara — GitHub (DINUM accessibility audit tool)
insights: Open source codebase for the Ara RGAA audit platform. Allows study of how RGAA criteria are structured in tooling. DINUM official repo.

title: Accessibility — React Docs
insights: Official React a11y guidance: programmatic focus, fragment as grouping element, accessible forms, aria-* support, semantic HTML. Includes note on React-specific deviations (className, htmlFor).

title: axe Accessibility Linter — VS Code Marketplace
insights: Free VS Code extension. Red wavy underline for violations. Tooltip with violation description. Checks React JSX, Angular, Vue, HTML. Pairs with @storybook/addon-a11y for full shift-left strategy.

title: Key Findings From WebAIM 2024 Screen Reader Survey — Colleen Gratzer / Medium
insights: Design-focused analysis of SR survey. Emphasis on headings, ARIA landmarks, form labels from SR users' perspective. Practical design implications.

title: Protecting Persons with Disabilities in a Digitised World — Privacy International
insights: Disability+privacy intersection analysis. Digitization of social support = accessibility opportunity + privacy risk. Automated decision systems bias against disability. Data about disability status = sensitive personal data under GDPR Article 9.

title: EU Accessibility Act in France: Law 2005-102 & RGAA — Quertum
insights: French Law 2005-102 as legal basis for RGAA. How EAA transposition extends obligations to private sector. French-specific enforcement context. CNIL role in data aspects.

title: European Accessibility Act & GDPR: What to Expect in 2025 — Quertum
insights: EAA mirrors GDPR enforcement model. Similar legislative structure. EAA seen as 'next GDPR' for scope and penalties. Privacy + accessibility intersection: inaccessible cookie banners create both GDPR and EAA violations simultaneously.

title: Automated Accessibility Testing with axe-core, Playwright & GitHub Actions
insights: Complete CI pipeline: install Playwright, run axe via @axe-core/playwright, generate JSON reports, create GitHub Issue on violations. Targets WCAG 2.1 AA by default. Practical GitHub Actions YAML included.

title: Manual vs Automated Accessibility Testing: A Strategic Comparison — TestParty
insights: Automated = 30-40% coverage, runs on every commit. Manual expert = monthly. User testing with disabled users = quarterly. Layered approach: testing pyramid for a11y. No single method catches all issues.

title: Screen Reader Testing Guide: NVDA, JAWS, VoiceOver — TestParty
insights: Testing matrix: NVDA (Windows, free, code issues), JAWS (Windows, enterprise, user experience), VoiceOver (macOS/iOS). Browse/Virtual mode vs Focus/Forms mode distinction critical. Test at least 2 screen readers.

title: WCAG 2.2 New Success Criteria Implementation Guide — TestParty
insights: Developer-focused implementation guide for all 9 new SC. Code examples for each criterion. Good companion to W3C understanding documents.

title: What Are All 87 WCAG 2.2 Success Criteria? — TestParty
insights: Complete flat list of all 87 criteria with level. Useful for building automated test matrices and mapping tools to criteria. Quick reference for team onboarding.

title: Building a Culture of Accessibility — TetraLogical (Apr 2024)
insights: Organizational approach: accessible onboarding, training for managers, inclusive policies. A11y as continuous culture not one-off audit. Practical org-level recommendations.

title: European Accessibility Act (EAA) Complete Compliance Guide 2025 — AllAccessible
insights: June 28 2025 deadline. EN 301 549 as conformance standard. WCAG 2.1 AA embedded. Spain: €1M max, Germany: €500k, France: €300k, Hungary: €1.26M. Exempts <10 employees companies. Pre-2025 archived content exempt.

title: WCAG 2.2 Complete Compliance Guide 2025 — AllAccessible
insights: 2025 implementation guidance for all 9 new WCAG 2.2 criteria with code examples. Practical focus on what changed from 2.1 to 2.2 for development teams.

title: WCAG 2.4.11 Focus Not Obscured: Complete Implementation Guide — AllAccessible
insights: Practical guide: CSS scroll-padding solution. scroll-padding-top = sticky header height. Conditional dismiss for cookie banners. scroll-margin alternative on individual elements.

title: WCAG 2.5.7 Dragging Movements Implementation Guide — AllAccessible
insights: Practical alternatives for dragging: numerical input next to slider, Up/Down keys for range, Delete button instead of drag-to-trash, explicit sort buttons instead of drag-to-reorder. All Level AA.

title: WCAG 2.5.8 Target Size (Minimum) Implementation Guide — AllAccessible
insights: 24x24 CSS px minimum OR offset spacing. Inline text links exempt. Hit area != visual size. Best practice 44x44. CSS padding often the easiest fix. Mobile native guidelines (Apple 44pt, Material 48dp) stricter than WCAG AA.

title: WCAG 3.0 Explained: What the Future Means for You — AudioEye
insights: WCAG 3.0 Working Draft Sept 2025. Replaces A/AA/AAA with Bronze/Silver/Gold. Shifts from binary pass/fail to scored outcomes. Still years from official recommendation. WCAG 2.2 remains the legal standard.

title: WCAG and PDF/UA in Focus for PDF Accessibility — axes4 (2024)
insights: 2024 analysis of how WCAG and PDF/UA overlap and differ. PDF/UA-2 (2024) as complete rewrite based on PDF 2.0. PAC 2024 checks WCAG 2.1 PDF criteria. Essential for enterprise document accessibility.

title: axe-core by Deque — Product Page
insights: Official product page. Key stat: 57% WCAG issue detection rate. Emphasizes zero-false-positives and CI integration. Links to all framework adapters.

title: Axe DevTools Linter — Deque
insights: IDE-time accessibility linting. 44 rules. Catches issues in JSX before render. Free personal edition for VS Code. CI/CD integration for PR blocking. Supports React, Angular, Vue, HTML, Markdown.

title: axe-core 4.5: First WCAG 2.2 Support — Deque Blog
insights: First version with WCAG 2.2 rules. Documents which of the 9 new SC are automatable. Shows how axe-core evolves to track standards updates. Key version for WCAG 2.2 adoption in CI pipelines.

title: EN 301 549 Compliance — Deque
insights: Practical guide to EN 301 549 compliance using axe-core. EN 301 549 v3.2.1 includes WCAG 2.1 AA. v4.1.1 planned 2026 for WCAG 2.2 and EAA. Deque tools mapped to EN 301 549 requirements.

title: WCAG 2.2 Checklist: Complete 2026 Compliance Guide — Level Access
insights: All 87 WCAG 2.2 success criteria organized by level. 32 Level A + 24 Level AA + 31 Level AAA. Practical implementation checklist format. Good QA gate reference.

title: @storybook/addon-a11y — npm
insights: Current version 8.6.0. Wraps axe-core. Installation: npx storybook add @storybook/addon-a11y. Config in .storybook/preview.js. Works with Test addon for automated background checks.

title: PDF/UA Standard Explained — PDF Tools
insights: PDF/UA (ISO 14289) structure and tagging requirements. PDF/UA-2 published 2024 based on PDF 2.0. All meaningful content in single structure tree. PAC 2024 checker from PDF/UA Foundation. Distinct from but complementary to WCAG.

title: Pope Tech — Enterprise Accessibility Platform (WAVE-powered)
insights: Enterprise-scale WAVE scanning. Automated Accessibility Score vs WebAIM Million baseline. Scheduled scans, dashboards, role-based reports. Used by universities, governments, large organizations. Updated WAVE engine in 2024.

title: ACR/VPAT FAQ — Section508.gov
insights: US government procurement guidance. Federal agencies must buy accessible ICT per Section 508. ACR/VPAT is the evaluation mechanism. Key for SaaS products targeting government customers.

title: Tree View Pattern — WAI-ARIA APG
insights: role='tree', role='treeitem', role='group' for sub-trees. Arrow keys: right expands, left collapses/moves up, down/up navigates. aria-expanded state. Complex pattern — use with care, simpler alternatives preferred.

title: Understanding SC 2.3.3: Animation from Interactions — WAI/W3C
insights: AAA level. Parallax scrolling, page transitions, progress indicator animations — all can be disabled via prefers-reduced-motion. Not limited to vestibular; covers all motion sickness triggers.

title: European Accessibility Act Compliance: What's Next for GDPR — ADA Site Compliance
insights: Both EAA and GDPR require cookie consent to be accessible (readable, keyboard-navigable, sufficient contrast). Inaccessible consent UI = dual violation. Clear legal intersection for SaaS products in EU.

title: WCAG 2.2 SC 3.3.7 Redundant Entry — Hong Kong Digital Policy Office
insights: Government implementation guide for SC 3.3.7. Auto-populate or present previously entered data as selectable option. Scope does not extend across sessions. Exception for security re-entry.
